Header.title: COORDINATION LAYER
Header.subtitle: Transformation of collective intent into technically comparable artifacts.

Header.title: ON-CHAIN REGISTRY
Header.subtitle: Universal source of technical truth. Verifiable scorecards published to the distributed ledger for ecosystem cross-coordination.

AtlasExplore.Footer: ATLAS Network is a decentralized reputation protocol for global technical coordination. All data is cryptographically anchored.

Header.title: SECURITY & QUALITY AUDIT
Header.subtitle: Continuous assessment of protocol integrity and technical standard compliance.

L7-WAF.title: Unfiltered Traffic Entry Point
L7-WAF.detail: Requests bypassing the L7 firewall rules, exposing internal endpoints to untrusted traffic.
L7-WAF.risk: Direct exploitation of application vulnerabilities via SQLi or XSS patterns.
L7-WAF.remediation: Activate L7-Shielding and enforce strict path validation.

DB-RLS.title: Incomplete Row Level Security
DB-RLS.detail: Data tables currently accessible via anonymous keys without proper RLS policies.
DB-RLS.risk: Unauthorized data exfiltration and potential PII exposure.
DB-RLS.remediation: Apply granular RLS policies to all core production tables.

SEC-HDR.title: Weak Security Header Policy
SEC-HDR.detail: Missing HSTS and CSP headers, increasing risk of MITM and injection attacks.
SEC-HDR.risk: Client-side exploitation through protocol downgrade and unverified scripts.

C-01.title: Core Consensus Logic Invariant
C-01.detail: Legacy consensus checks allowed out-of-order execution in edge cases.
C-01.risk: State divergence between concurrent network nodes.
C-01.remediation: Implemented atomic ordering in the coordination kernel.

C-02.title: Contract Structure Unification
C-02.detail: Multiple entry points for Vara and Rootstock contracts create sync overhead.
C-02.risk: Increased attack surface and maintenance complexity.
C-02.remediation: Refactoring into a unified multi-chain bridge controller.

M-01.title: Rate Limiting Bypass
M-01.detail: Redis middleware configuration allows bursting beyond safe thresholds.
M-01.risk: Potential resource exhaustion (DoS) during high-traffic events.
M-01.remediation: Configuring sliding window rate limiting in Upstash.

M-02.title: Secret Manager Leakage
M-02.detail: Environment variables being logged during startup sequences.
M-02.risk: Exposure of sensitive API keys to diagnostic logs.
M-02.remediation: Implemented masked logging for all identified secrets.

B-01.title: Legacy API Deprecation
B-01.detail: Public access still active for v1.0 endpoints scheduled for removal.
B-01.risk: Users continuing to use insecure legacy interfaces.
B-01.remediation: Apply 301 redirects to V2.0 and disable v1.0 routes.

B-02.title: Audit Trace Inconsistency
B-02.detail: Log rotation missing on development infrastructure nodes.
B-02.risk: Potential disk pressure and loss of historical audit context.
B-02.remediation: Activated automated S3 log persistence and rotation policy.

Header.subtitle: Access to core buffers, security dry-run simulations, and JSON invariant validation. Direct bridge to kernel with real-time diagnostic visualization.

proof.title: REGISTRY PROOF GENERATED
proof.verifiedBy: Verified by Andromeda Core v1.0 Functional-Validator

alerts.requiredIdentity: REQUIRED IDENTITY: Please connect your wallet to sign this registry entry.

Finding.remediationPending: Remediation strategy pending system architecture review.

L7-WAF.title: Layer 7 Firewall (WAF) Integration
L7-WAF.detail: Andromeda Shield logic monitors incoming L7 traffic for SQLi/XSS signatures.
L7-WAF.risk: Exposure to injection attacks if perimeter defenses are bypassed.
L7-WAF.remediation: Enforced strict WAF rules and real-time threat monitoring.

DB-RLS.title: Database Row Level Security (RLS)
DB-RLS.detail: Data access layer integrity via Supabase RLS policies.
DB-RLS.risk: Unauthorized data exposure if RLS is disabled on public schemas.
DB-RLS.remediation: Validated all tables against authenticated-only access policies.

SEC-HDR.title: Perimeter Security Headers
SEC-HDR.detail: Modern HTTP security headers (HSTS, CSP, XFO, XCTO).
SEC-HDR.risk: Susceptibility to MITM and Cross-Site scripting attacks.
SEC-HDR.remediation: Applied strict security policy headers in next.config.js.

C-01.title: Production Credentials Exposure in Repository
C-01.detail: File contains keys with full privileges: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, PINATA_JWT, UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN.
C-01.risk: RLS bypass, total IPFS access, and total control over cache/rate-limiting.
C-01.remediation: Credentials have been rotated and a security proxy has been enabled for sensitive access.

C-02.title: Simulated Vara Network Integration
C-02.detail: The code uses hardcoded simulation blocks and does not make real calls to @gear-js/api.
C-02.risk: False sense of on-chain immutability. Generated hashes are artificial.
C-02.remediation: Migrating to real @gear-js/api with support for Vara Mainnet.

M-01.title: Validation Engine Redundancy
M-01.detail: RegistryService uses a simplified version of the validator, ignoring the external Invariants system.
M-01.risk: Publishing scorecards that violate already programmed logical invariants.
M-01.remediation: Unifying the validation engine under the @andromeda/auto-validator package.

M-02.title: Nonce Logic Weakness
M-02.detail: Lack of persistence for used nonces. Risk of Replay Attacks during network failures.
M-02.risk: Reuse of signatures within a 5-minute window.
M-02.remediation: Implemented persistent set of burned nonces in Redis with replication.

B-01.title: Inconsistency in Contract Structure
B-01.detail: Multiple folders for Vara contracts can cause confusion during compilation.
B-01.risk: Accidental deployment of stubs instead of real contracts.
B-01.remediation: Pending unification of all contracts in the workspace root.

B-02.title: Lack of Coverage in Diagnostic Route
B-02.detail: Does not verify connectivity with Pinata or Vara Network status.
B-02.remediation: Added health-checks for IPFS (Pinata) and Gear API Gateway.

Header.title: LOGIC ARCHITECTURE AUDIT
Header.subtitle: Multilevel scrutiny of invariants and consensus integrity.

0.name: TIER 0: CONFIG & IDL
0.description: Environment variables and Gear/Vara contract schema integrity.

1.name: TIER 1: API COMPLIANCE
1.description: Type validation and security of core system endpoints.

2.name: TIER 2: PROTOCOL CONSENSUS
2.description: Cross-layer synchrony and stability of the Vara consensus network.

3.name: TIER 3: INVARIANT INTEGRITY
3.description: Formal mathematical verification and deterministic logic validation.

About.title: About ATLAS Verification Levels
About.description: The ATLAS Paper defines 4 levels of verification certainty. Higher levels represent stronger cryptographic and consensus guarantees. Level 3 (Math Proof) provides maximum immutability through algorithmic verification, while Level 0 (Self-Attestation) serves as the entry point for new contributions.

Hero.tagline: AVIP v2.0 — Andromeda Verifiable Immutable Proof
Hero.subtitle: Andromeda Core is the infrastructure layer for portable, cryptographically verified builder reputation across DAOs, marketplaces and Web3 ecosystems.

step1.title: Ingest
step1.desc: Atlas Engine pulls governance data from Rootstock, Arbitrum, Optimism, Snapshot and more.

step2.title: Verify
step2.desc: AVIP v2.0 applies Shannon entropy, asymmetric decay and multi-chain scoring to every milestone.

step3.title: Anchor
step3.desc: Proofs are stored immutably on Vara Network and IPFS. Portable, verifiable, forever.
